title: chabertia sepium
intro: Chabertia sepium is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa and Asia, also known as the "African wormseed" or "dodonea." It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a natural anthelmintic, which is an agent that expels intestinal worms. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of parasitic infections, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Chabertia sepium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
